summary: Kenneth Suna discusses dividend investing strategy and how to find dividend-paying stocks/ETFs. The content focuses on long-term wealth building through dividend compounding and provides a research hack of examining top 10 holdings in dividend ETFs. This is general investment education with no relevance to Brian Norton's Premium Desk options-selling strategy.
takeaways: Dividend stocks compound over decades; small initial payouts ($12) grow significantly over time
CHD dividend ETF pays approximately 3.93% annual dividend or $14 per share
Research hack: examine top 10 holdings of dividend ETFs to identify individual dividend-paying companies
ETF search method: type desired theme plus 'ETF' (e.g., 'robotics ETF', 'artificial intelligence ETF') to find relevant funds
Young investors should be disproportionately heavy in technology stocks while maintaining some dividend allocation
Dividend strategy requires decades of compounding and reinvestment, not immediate returns
ETFs provide a lower-research alternative for investors uncomfortable analyzing individual stocks
title: How to Find the Best Dividend Stocks #shorts
